## **nature synthesis**
**Article**
## **Miniaturization of popular reactions from the medicinal chemists’ toolbox for ultrahigh-throughput experimentation**
Received: 17 August 2022 **Nathan Gesmundo[1,4] , Kevin Dykstra[2] , James L. Douthwaite[3] , Yu-Ting Kao[3] , Ruheng Zhao[3] , Babak Mahjour[3] , Ron Ferguson[2] ,** Accepted: 22 May 2023 **Spencer Dreher[2] , Bérengère Sauvagnat[1,5] , Josep Saurí[1,6] & Tim Cernak[1,3,7]** Published online: 22 June 2023 Check for updates Miniaturization is a tactic employed in many technologies to accelerate discovery through parallelized data collection. The miniaturization of chemical synthesis to the limits of chemoanalytical and bioanalytical detection could accelerate drug discovery by increasing the amount of experimental data collected per milligram of material consumed. Here we demonstrate the miniaturization of popular reactions used in drug discovery such as reductive amination, _N_ -alkylation, _N_ -Boc ( _tert_ -butyloxycarbonyl) deprotection and Suzuki coupling for utilization in 1.2 µl reaction droplets.
methods_summary: In ultraHTE[8] , a typical reaction volume is ~1 l, and solvents and reagents generally have a low enough vapour pressure that they can be handled without substantial evaporation during experiment setup. Our development of a Suzuki coupling protocol for ultraHTE was inspired by prior reports detailing the nanoscale us…
